description: AA-09.2 failure-domain compiler, restart topology hooks, and recovery authority rules.

failure_domain_model.description: Failure domains partition supervised regions into restart-independent groups with explicit boundaries.

domain_types.description: Failure in this domain does not propagate to siblings. Independent restart.
Failure cascades to all members of the linked group. Group restarts together.
Failure escalates to the parent domain after local restart budget is exhausted.

domain_properties.description: Failure domain boundaries must be declared at region creation, not inferred.
Every supervised region belongs to exactly one failure domain.
The parent-child relationship between failure domains forms a DAG.
Domain membership and boundaries are queryable at runtime for diagnostics.

restart_topology.description: Topology hooks that govern how regions within a failure domain are restarted.

topologies.description: Only the failed region is restarted. Siblings continue unaffected.
When one region in the group fails, all are restarted together.
The failed region and all regions started after it are restarted.
After local restart budget exhaustion, failure escalates to parent domain.

hooks.description: Invoked before restart: checkpoint state, fence I/O, narrow authority.
Invoked after restart: validate state, re-register obligations, widen authority.
Invoked when failure escalates to parent domain.
Invoked when a domain is tombstoned after exhausting all recovery options.

recovery_authority_rules.description: Rules governing how capability tokens and authority are handled during recovery.

rules.description: On crash, all non-OBSERVE capabilities are immediately revoked for the recovering region.
After successful recovery, capabilities are restored incrementally: OBSERVE first, then DECIDE after shadow validation.
If restart budget is exhausted, all capabilities for the domain are permanently revoked.
When a domain escalates, the parent inherits the child's revocation set.
Every authority change during recovery is logged with domain_id, old_caps, new_caps, and reason.
No ambient authority is available during any recovery phase. All actions require explicit tokens.

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate failure domain model properties and domain types
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa092 cargo test --test failure_domain_contract domain -- --nocapture
rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa092 cargo test --test failure_domain_contract topology -- --nocapture

smoke_scenarios.description: Validate recovery authority rules and capability transitions
smoke_scenarios.command: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa092 cargo test --test failure_domain_contract authority -- --nocapture
